zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de la Lynde II於1180年出生在英國英格蘭多瑟特文特爾布恩 可倫斯同時，他的父親Robert de la Lynde I40歲，母親Mrs Robert De La Lynde31歲。他於1214年與Alice Advice of Outwell在英國多瑟特文特爾布恩 可倫斯同結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1255年之後在他的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Lynde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Lind
meanings: Scandinavian, English, and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived by a lime tree, Scandinavian and Middle English lind. As a Swedish name, it is often of ornamental origin.
North German and Danish: habitational name from any of various places called with Lind ‘lime tree’.
South German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a gentle, mild, tolerant man, from Middle High German lint, linde, Yiddish lind ‘gentle, mild’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de la Lynde II was born in 1180, in Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert de la Lynde I, was 40 and his mother, Mrs Robert De La Lynde, was 31. He married Alice Advice of Outwell in 1214, in Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died after 1255, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de la Lynde II est né en 1180, à Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de la Lynde I, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Mrs Robert De La Lynde, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Alice Advice of Outwell en 1214, à Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé après 1255, dans sa ville natale.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de la Lynde IIが1180年にイギリスイギリスドールセトウィンテルボウルン クレンストーンで生まれた時，父Robert de la Lynde Iは40歳，母Mrs Robert De La Lyndeは31歳であった。1214年にイギリスドールセトウィンテルボウルン クレンストーンでAlice Advice of Outwellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1255年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de la Lynde II(이)가 1180년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 윈터보우른 클렌스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de la Lynde I(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Robert De La Lynde(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1214년 영국 도르세트 윈터보우른 클렌스톤에서 Alice Advice of Outwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1255년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de la Lynde II родился в 1180 г. в местности винтирбурн кленстоун, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert de la Lynde I было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Robert De La Lynde, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Advice of Outwell в 1214 г. в местности винтирбурн кленстоун, дорсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер после 1255 г. в его родном городе.
